IOH/IOL Max

Measures IO output current drive capability and resistance vs. DS setting. IOL = sink current (output low); IOH = source current (output high). R_Low / R_High = output resistance derived from VOL / VOH under load.

IO State After POR

Verifies IO pin state and direction after the chip initialisation process. Shows Pull Mode, IO State (High/Low), and IO Direction (Output/Input) for each temperature and skew condition.

VOH/VOL

Output voltage under load: VOH = output high (must be ≥ spec min); VOL = output low (must be ≤ spec max).
